---
title: "Water"
description: "Fresh water across Canada — the major lakes and how much of each province is water"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os, json
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import CANADA_COLOR, get_data_date
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_map, choropleth_groups_map, choropleth_categorical, add_city_markers

MAP_CONFIG = {"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True,
              "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]}
prov_geo_path, prov_csv = "data/geo/prov_2021.geojson", "data/geo/statcan_prov_geography.csv"
```

## A country of fresh water

Canada holds close to **20% of the world's fresh water** and **more lake area than any other country on Earth**. The map below highlights the **40 largest lakes**, shaded by surface area: the shared **Great Lakes** along the southern border, and the great northern lakes — **Great Bear** and **Great Slave** in the Northwest Territories, and **Athabasca**, **Winnipeg** and **Reindeer** across the Prairies and Canadian Shield. Hover any lake for its name and surface area; zoom and pan to explore.

```{python}
#| echo: false
lakes_geo, lakes_csv = "data/geo/lakes_major.geojson", "data/geo/lakes_major.csv"
if os.path.exists(lakes_geo) and os.path.exists(lakes_csv):
    lg = json.load(open(lakes_geo))
    ld = pd.read_csv(lakes_csv, dtype={"lakeid": str})
    fig = choropleth_map(lg, ld, "lakeid", "area_km2", name_col="name",
        colorbar_title="Surface area", colorscale="Blues", value_suffix=" km²", value_fmt=",.0f",
        log=True, center={"lat": 59, "lon": -98}, zoom=2.4,
        source_note="Source: Natural Resources Canada, Atlas of Canada 1:1,000,000 waterbodies. The 40 largest named natural lakes touching Canada (shared Great Lakes included; reservoirs excluded). Surface area approximate, from 1:1M-scale outlines; colour on a log scale.")
    add_city_markers(fig)
    fig.show(config=MAP_CONFIG)
else:
    print("Lakes data not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_geography`.")
```

### Fresh water by province

Another way to see it: how much of each province or territory is actually *covered* by inland water. It varies widely — nearly **15% of Ontario and Manitoba** is fresh water, against around 2% of Alberta and British Columbia. Prince Edward Island has effectively none.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(prov_geo_path) and os.path.exists(prov_csv):
    prov_geo = json.load(open(prov_geo_path))
    pg = pd.read_csv(prov_csv, dtype={"pruid": str})
    fig = choropleth_map(prov_geo, pg, "pruid", "pct_freshwater", name_col="name",
        colorbar_title="% fresh water", colorscale="Blues", value_suffix="%", value_fmt=".1f",
        center={"lat": 62, "lon": -96}, zoom=2.3,
        source_note="Source: Natural Resources Canada / Statistics Canada land and freshwater area. Inland lakes & rivers as a share of total area.")
    fig.show(config=MAP_CONFIG)
else:
    print("Freshwater data not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_geography`.")
```

## Download data

- [Major lakes — surface area (CSV)](../data/geo/lakes_major.csv) — NRCan Atlas of Canada 1:1,000,000 waterbodies
- [Land & freshwater area by province (CSV)](../data/geo/statcan_prov_geography.csv) — NRCan / Statistics Canada
